title_en: Khadijah bint Khuwaylid: The First Believer
title_id: Khadijah binti Khuwailid: Mukmin yang Pertama
content_en:
Khadijah bint Khuwaylid stands at the threshold of Islamic history as the first human being to affirm faith in the divine message that Muhammad received in Cave Hira. Her position in the Islamic narrative is unique: she was not merely the Prophet's wife but his first companion in faith, his primary emotional support during the most vulnerable period of his prophetic mission, and the woman who provided the material stability that enabled him to devote himself fully to a mission that would otherwise have been economically ruinous.

Mahmood Ahmad Ghadanfar's portrait of Khadijah draws on the hadith sources with genuine affection and scholarly care. Born into a wealthy Quraysh family, Khadijah had been widowed twice before she encountered Muhammad — then employed as her trade agent — whose exceptional trustworthiness and moral character impressed her enough to propose marriage. The marriage, which she initiated through an intermediary, reversed the usual social convention and reflected her confidence and independence of mind.

The marriage between Khadijah and Muhammad was by every indication one of profound love and mutual respect. For twenty-five years — from his marriage at twenty-five to her death when he was approximately fifty — Muhammad was exclusively married to Khadijah and took no other wife. His subsequent marriages, after her death, should be understood partly against this background: he experienced and valued the depth of a committed monogamous partnership and only moved beyond it for specific religious, social, and political purposes after Khadijah was gone.

When the revelation came, Khadijah's response was the first and most essential act of support the prophetic mission received from any human being. Her consoling words — 'Allah will never disgrace you; you uphold kinship, speak truth, help the weak, honor guests, and support the afflicted' — were not merely domestic comfort but a theological affirmation: a person of such character must be under divine grace. Her response reflects both her love for her husband and her independent moral insight into the relationship between righteous character and divine favor.

Khadijah's own faith was not merely passive assent. She accompanied the Prophet to Waraqa ibn Nawfal, lent the weight of her social standing to the early Muslim community at a time when community standing was a matter of survival, and used her wealth to support the early Muslims through the years of poverty and persecution. The Prophet's ongoing remembrance of her after her death — honoring her friends, becoming emotional at the mention of her name — suggests that Khadijah remained the emotional center of his life even after her death. His description of her as 'the best of the women of her time' and his statement that Allah had not given him better than her reflect a depth of continuing love that centuries have found moving.
content_id:
Khadijah binti Khuwailid berdiri di ambang sejarah Islam sebagai manusia pertama yang menegaskan iman dalam pesan ilahi yang Muhammad ﷺ terima di Gua Hira. Posisinya dalam narasi Islam adalah unik: ia bukan hanya istri Nabi ﷺ tetapi sahabat pertamanya dalam iman, dukungan emosional utamanya selama periode paling rentan dari misi kenabiannya, dan perempuan yang menyediakan kestabilan material yang memungkinkan beliau mendedikasikan dirinya sepenuhnya kepada sebuah misi yang jika tidak akan secara ekonomi menghancurkan.

Potret Mahmood Ahmad Ghadanfar tentang Khadijah mengandalkan sumber-sumber hadis dengan kasih sayang yang tulus dan kehati-hatian keilmuan. Lahir dalam keluarga Quraisy yang kaya, Khadijah telah dua kali menjadi janda sebelum ia bertemu Muhammad ﷺ — yang saat itu bekerja sebagai agen perdagangannya — yang kepercayaan dan karakter moralnya yang luar biasa membuat beliau terkesan untuk melamarnya. Pernikahan tersebut, yang ia mulai melalui perantara, membalik konvensi sosial yang biasa dan mencerminkan kepercayaan diri dan kemandirian pikirannya.

Pernikahan antara Khadijah dan Muhammad ﷺ berdasarkan setiap indikasi adalah pernikahan yang penuh cinta dan saling menghormati yang mendalam. Selama dua puluh lima tahun — dari pernikahannya pada usia dua puluh lima hingga kematiannya ketika beliau berusia sekitar lima puluh tahun — Muhammad ﷺ secara eksklusif menikah dengan Khadijah dan tidak mengambil istri lain. Pernikahan-pernikahan berikutnya beliau, setelah kematiannya, harus dipahami sebagian dalam latar belakang ini: beliau mengalami dan menghargai kedalaman kemitraan monogami yang berkomitmen dan hanya bergerak melampaui itu untuk tujuan-tujuan agama, sosial, dan politik tertentu setelah Khadijah pergi.

Ketika wahyu datang, respons Khadijah adalah tindakan dukungan pertama dan paling penting yang diterima misi kenabian dari manusia mana pun. Kata-kata penghiburannya — 'Allah tidak akan pernah menghinakan engkau; engkau menyambung silaturahmi, berkata jujur, membantu yang lemah, memuliakan tamu, dan mendukung yang tertimpa kesulitan' — bukan sekadar kenyamanan domestik tetapi penegasan teologis: seseorang dengan karakter seperti itu pasti berada di bawah karunia ilahi. Responsnya mencerminkan baik cintanya kepada suaminya maupun wawasan moral independennya tentang hubungan antara karakter yang saleh dan kasih sayang ilahi.

Iman Khadijah sendiri bukan sekadar persetujuan pasif. Ia menemani Nabi ﷺ ke Waraqah bin Nawfal, meminjamkan bobot kedudukan sosialnya kepada komunitas Muslim awal pada saat kedudukan komunitas adalah soal kelangsungan hidup, dan menggunakan kekayaannya untuk mendukung kaum Muslim awal melalui tahun-tahun kemiskinan dan penganiayaan. Kenangan Nabi ﷺ yang terus-menerus tentang Khadijah setelah kematiannya — menghormati teman-temannya, menjadi emosional ketika namanya disebut — menunjukkan bahwa Khadijah tetap menjadi pusat emosional hidupnya bahkan setelah kematiannya. Deskripsinya tentang Khadijah sebagai 'sebaik-baik wanita di masanya' dan pernyataannya bahwa Allah tidak memberinya yang lebih baik darinya mencerminkan kedalaman cinta yang berlanjut yang selama berabad-abad telah menyentuh hati.
content_ar:
Khadijah bint Khuwaylid stands at the threshold of Islamic history as the first human being to affirm faith in the divine message that Muhammad received in Cave Hira. Her position in the Islamic narrative is unique: she was not merely النبي ﷺ's wife but his first companion in faith, his primary emotional support during the most vulnerable period of his prophetic mission, and the woman who provided the material stability that enabled him to devote himself fully to a mission that would otherwise have been economically ruinous.

Mahmood Ahmad Ghadanfar's portrait of Khadijah draws on the الحديث sources with genuine affection and scholarly care. Born into a wealthy Quraysh family, Khadijah had been widowed twice before she encountered Muhammad — then employed as her trade agent — whose exceptional trustworthiness and moral character impressed her enough to propose marriage. The marriage, which she initiated through an intermediary, reversed the usual social convention and reflected her confidence and independence of mind.

The marriage between Khadijah and Muhammad was by every indication one of profound love and mutual respect. For twenty-five years — from his marriage at twenty-five to her death when he was approximately fifty — Muhammad was exclusively married to Khadijah and took no other wife. His subsequent marriages, after her death, should be understood partly against this background: he experienced and valued the depth of a committed monogamous partnership and only moved beyond it for specific religious, social, and political purposes after Khadijah was gone.

When the revelation came, Khadijah's response was the first and most essential act of support the prophetic mission received from any human being. Her consoling words — 'الله will never disgrace you; you uphold kinship, speak truth, help the weak, honor guests, and support the afflicted' — were not merely domestic comfort but a theological affirmation: a person of such character must be under divine grace. Her response reflects both her love for her husband and her independent moral insight into the relationship between righteous character and divine favor.

Khadijah's own faith was not merely passive assent. She accompanied النبي ﷺ to Waraqa ibn Nawfal, lent the weight of her social standing to the early Muslim community at a time when community standing was a matter of survival, and used her wealth to support the early Muslims through the years of poverty and persecution. النبي ﷺ's ongoing remembrance of her after her death — honoring her friends, becoming emotional at the mention of her name — suggests that Khadijah remained the emotional center of his life even after her death. His description of her as 'the best of the women of her time' and his statement that الله had not given him better than her reflect a depth of continuing love that centuries have found moving.
